The error is "end bit of signal is out of message length"  It will allow if it's Intel format.  Or if the signal length is reduced to 8 bits.      Then the next signal, which should be starting at bit 16 (or byte 2, bit 0), in fact is sent starting at byte 1.    Am I misunderstanding something basic, or does this just no

issue_title: Very basic CAN database problem (3.2.2)  "end bit of signal is out of message length"
author: B2B-Rohit
text_preview: when working with motorola type your end bit becomes the start bit for the busmaster and your start bit becomes the end bit.  for example if your signal is 16 bit long and starts at the 0th bit in intel hex you should provide "BYTE Index" to 1 and "Start Bit" to 0. This feature is pretty confusing though I guess we have to work this way for now.

issue_title: ISO 14299 incorrect diagnostic message send
author: dskibarko
text_preview: The UDS messages should by a 8 bytes of CAN data allways. Not used bytes should be filled by paging data (0xAA value)/ It is a strong requirements but BusMaster can't support it.  For example command "Default diagnostic session" supported only this **(0x02)  0x10 0x01** but not support strong ISO 8 bytes data  **(0x02) 0x10 0x01  0xAA 0xAA 0xAA 0xAA 0xAA**.  I try to do manualy and observe strange multiframe transmitted instead of my data. Behavior is on the picture  ![BusMasterOpenSessionIssue]

text_preview: Is the error continuously being transmitted? I.E.: is the time for the error message incrementing, or is it once off. If once off then you should be able to clear the message window and it won't return.    If it does then you either have a hardware or software configuration issue.    A Bit error is:  ```When the bit received is not the same as the bit transmitted. But since this project has nobody to merge the pull requests, is there any point to upstream? License is GPL, so that states that any changes made should be contributed back here. Unless I misunderstood.

issue_title: BUSMASTER Activity
author: vguttmann
text_preview: @arihannu So this is licensed under the GPLv3, so as long as you fulfill a couple of requirements, you're good to go. First, no sublicensing (e.g. changing to a more prohibitive license).  What you're referring to is the clause on modifications. You're good regarding that as long as you make the code of your fork openly accessible (since the upstream project can't be required to merge your contributions). Just fork the project on GitHub, and keep access open, and that should be it.  If you plan

author: yegorich
text_preview: You'll need for example a USB-to-CAN adapter attached to mcp2515. This way you can communincate with Arduino via a CAN bus.

issue_title: Interfacing Busmaster with CAN module like mcp2515 and arduino
author: krish97git
text_preview: Don't I need specific drivers for BUSMASTER to successfully run it using arduino and MCP2515  I dint get any drivers for that, so arises the question.

issue_title: Interfacing Busmaster with CAN module like mcp2515 and arduino
author: yegorich
text_preview: AFAIK you cannot attach Arduino directly to BUSMASTER. So you if have an application that is using CAN on Arduino and Arduino interfaces with CAN through MCP2515 (SPI bus) and you want to use BUSMASTER to sniff on the CAN bus then you need an interface already supported by BUSMASTER. Like for example this [one](http://www.visionsystems.de/produkte/usb-can-plus-usb-can-plus-iso.html).

issue_title: Interfacing Busmaster with CAN module like mcp2515 and arduino
author: viperidae

text_preview: What do you want to achieve? Do you have a CAN program running on imx6 and you want to debug CAN communication or do you want to use imx6 board as a CAN-to-PC converter? issue_title: BUSMASTER switch to RERO Development
author: Nit2Nat
text_preview: @jogo- The Busmaster development team’s “wish-list” for usability & functionality features keep getting bigger based on constant feedback from the users. :)    Since we haven’t had enough helping hands via code contributions, implementing a lot of them with a small team was quite an effort. Hence the focus was to prioritize stability, fix any major bugs and try to implement the less effort-intensive features over a period of time.

issue_title: BUSMASTER switch to RERO Development
author: Nit2Nat
text_preview: With the release of v3.2.x, idea was to ensure stability on Win10 and the plug-in architecture that allows users to create plug-in extensions to use with open source tool. This is the reason for no new releases since v3.2.2, but the project is very much alive :)    And with contributions from keen community developers, it would be possible to implement the wish-list much faster.

issue_title: BUSMASTER switch to RERO Development
author: yegorich
text_preview: @ETAS-Nithin Thanks for an update. I'm glad to hear that the project is still alive. But one cannot see the progress from outside as you don't push single commits. These big commits with thousands of changes are very contra productive for the people willing to contribute. Besides I see 10 almost untouched PRs.    Making CMake working again and enabling other Visual Studio versions would be also very helpful.    AppVeyor support would be very much appreciated.

issue_title: BUSMASTER switch to RERO Development
author: jogo-
text_preview: @ETAS-Nithin I totally agree with @yegorich suggestions    > Making CMake working again and enabling other Visual Studio versions would be also very helpful.  > AppVeyor support would be very much appreciated.    This will certainly and definitely help in getting more contributions from the community developers.

issue_title: Wrong timing for TX Messages on CAN
author: rickyi89
text_preview: All the 3.x versions seems to be affected by a timing issue for what concerns TX messages, even in simulation mode. If I set a periodic message on CAN, for example 2000ms, it is delayed by a 1.45 factor, which means that the message is sent about every 2.9s instead 2s (5.7s if period is 4s, 145ms if period is 100ms and so on). I've found this issue in all our company computers, all running Windows 10.  RX messages show correct timings.

author: nostar
text_preview: Ah, I see.  Looks like they typo'd the function name in the header file https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/blob/master/Sources/BUSMASTER/Application/SimulatedSystems/include/BMJ1939Defines.h    ```  GCC_EXTERN UINT GCC_EXPORT SetTimout(BYTE, UINT);  ```    If your comfortable with the Busmaster source, you can fix the typo in the header and rebuild LibWrapper_J1939.a and replace the installed version, using the batch script in /Sources/BUSMASTER/Application/SimulatedSystems/include/ .  Other

issue_title: How to transmit slave response after reception of header part from master
author: RBEI-busmaster
text_preview: To transmit Slave response for a header request, add message in 'LIN->Transmit Window' and select 'Message Type' as 'Slave'. Use 'Data Byte View' or 'Signal Details' view to update data bytes.    By default, 'Message Type' will be selected as 'Slave'.

issue_title: No Ribbon on Win7 (Japanese Workstation)
author: Nit2Nat
text_preview: It was retained since we were still evaluating Japanese language support. We have closed this topic and from the subsequent version, the JPN folder will not be part of the release. English text will be displayed for all OS languages.

issue_title: No Ribbon on Win7 (Japanese Workstation)
author: ghost
text_preview: Thank you for your feedback. I suppose , "no support for Japanese language" is much better than "not working at Japanese environment at all". I also support your decision.

issue_title: No Ribbon on Win7 (Japanese Workstation)
author: Nit2Nat
text_preview: you are welcome and thank you for supporting the decision. Over last few years, we had feedback from quite a few users of Japanese OS and other language users that they are comfortable working with English text version rather than having issues/bugs associated with dedicated language versions.     When we integrated Windows 10 x64 ENG support, it was planned to gradually discontinue other language versions.

It shows "driver selection failed" when use with VCI V4.    link to VCI V4: https://www.ixxat.com/support/file-and-documents-download/drivers/vci-v4-driver  link to VCI V3: https://www.ixxat.com/support/file-and-documents-download/drivers/vci-v3-driver-download

issue_title: Busmaster 3.1.0 failed to recognise IXXAT CAN-to-USB V2 under VCI driver V4
author: jbiefm
text_preview: I have the same issue.  For compatibility issues with other code, I would prefer to use VCI V4.  Is there an idea whether and/or when this will be addressed, i.e. VCI V4 will be supported/recognized?

issue_title: Busmaster 3.1.0 failed to recognise IXXAT CAN-to-USB V2 under VCI driver V4
author: bodegaard
text_preview: My new Windows 10 machine will refuse to boot if there's unsigned drivers installed. Even getting an unsigned driver installed in the first place is a hastle. The problem reoccurs every time windows does an update and I've got to do a workaround every time to get the signature check re-disabled.    VCI V4 driver has a signed version, however VCI V3 does not. This means that I cannot use my USB-CAN Compact with busmaster without this work around.    I'm currently using Busmaster 3.2.2

issue_title: Busmaster 3.1.0 failed to recognise IXXAT CAN-to-USB V2 under VCI driver V4
author: polyene
text_preview: I'm also having this issue. My new Windows 10 PC with secure boot refuses to boot with the VCI3 driver installed. All other programs that I have that were designed for VCI3 seems to work fine with the VCI4 driver, so I don't know why BusMaster doesn't.     This seems like a critical issue for anyone using the IXXAT interfaces, since you're sooner or later forced to upgrade to VCI4.    I'm also using Busmaster 3.2.2.

issue_title: Busmaster 3.1.0 failed to recognise IXXAT CAN-to-USB V2 under VCI driver V4
author: bodegaard
text_preview: I've found a work around for now! There's a specific version that works. see the faq.    "    > Q: Why the VCI V3.5.4.4543 does not run with the Windows 10?  > Please deactivate the Secure-Boot in the BIOS or use the "VCI V4.0.482.0 Attestation Signed" or use the VCI 3.5.2.4268.    "    [link](https://www.ixxat.com/support/faq-and-tips/driver-and-api/vci-driver-installation-faq)

issue_title: Busmaster 3.1.0 failed to recognise IXXAT CAN-to-USB V2 under VCI driver V4
author: polyene
text_preview: Thank you bodegaard, the workaround worked fine for me!    But I'm still hoping to see VCI4 support in the future to make the installation process easier.

issue_title: Peak virtual network always created when Peak CAN driver selected and never removed
author: nostar
text_preview: It's pretty obvious.  Open Busmaster, select Peak hardware.  A virtual network is created by busmaster before any peak hardware has been discovered.  This has been the case since Busmaster 1.0.    I recently spoke with Nithin on the phone, and he led me to believe that peak hardware was not available to anyone at ETAS.  If this is the case, then you cannot replicate this bug.

issue_title: No ability to change hardware configuration on a single channel device
author: nostar
text_preview: In 3.1 the 'Channel Selection' was renamed to 'Channel Configuration', and what used to be 'Channel Configuration' is now an advanced button on the channel selection dialog.  The problem is that the Channel Configuration button is not active for a single channel device, so now there is no way to change configuration settings, such as baud rate, on a single channel device.  This is true for Peak and Vector hardware, and IXXAT hardware according to #1016

issue_title: J1939 Mux Ids are not handled.
author: Squall-DA
text_preview: J1939 allows for the transmission of mux-id bytes so that one byte of the CAN message can contain different data based on the current mux-id. Currently if a muxed CAN message is received by BUSMASTER, signals are populated with data from different mux-ids.

issue_title: STCAN_MSG alignment broken between DIL and Node Simulation
author: nostar
text_preview: The addition of the timeStamp member to the STCAN_MSG class on the node simulation side, broke the ability to access the isCanfd (m_bCANFD) member from the DIL side.  I have added CAN-FD support to the CAN_PEAK_USB DIL, but the STCAN_MSG class byte alignment needs to be equal on both sides in order for all of the members to be accessible from both sides.  The compiler fixes the alignment differences between bool and unsigned char for isRtr and isExtended, but I changed them anyway just for good

issue_title: Busmaster .log to .asc conversion bug (timestamps)
author: CANberryy
text_preview: Hi, I noticed another bug in the conversion of .log into .asc files with the implemented Konverter.  The timestamps in the .log file don't match the timestamps in the .asc file, when exceeding 1 hour. After every hour (3600s, 7200s, ...) in the .log file the konverted result is +60s ( 3660s, 7260s +60s = 7320s, ...)  I checked it with several .log-files, it's always the same. Doesn't matter if one large .log file or several parts.  Example: .log-file: ... 00:59:59:9905 Rx 1 0x306 s 8 8E 2A C4 2A

issue_title: Busmaster .log to .asc conversion bug (timestamps)
author: WillieShih
text_preview: I have same problem. I found the source code Log_Asc_Parser.y have error.  https://github.com/rbei-etas/busmaster/blob/master/Sources/BUSMASTER/Format%20Converter/LogAscConverter/Log_Asc_Parser.y  The fourteenth row of the function nGetAscTimeStamp original unTotalSec = nHours_3660 + nMin \* 60 + nSec; correct unTotalSec = nHours_3600 + nMin \* 60 + nSec;  Please release the Patch update this issue. Thanks

issue_title: Busmaster .log to .asc conversion bug (timestamps)
author: CANberryy
text_preview: Yea that's it! Thank you very much!

issue_title: Filter
author: RBEI-Prathiba
text_preview: To apply Filter on log, follow the below steps: 1. Configure filters using 'CAN->Filter Configuration' 2. Apply the filter to log using 'Configure Logging' window. 'Configure Logging' window can be launched via menu 'CAN->Logging->Configure' 3. Enable Filters for logging using 'CAN->Logging->Enable Filters'  Similar way apply the filters for Replay also. For more details please refer help 'CAN->Configuration->Log and Replay' and 'CAN->Enable/Disable Filter'

issue_title: Busmaster wont start in CAN FD mode with ES523 consistently
author: nostar

It only worked after changing txDelayCompensation from OFF to ON.  Today I'm trying to start it in CAN-FD mode and it refuses to do so, it just keeps starting in standard CAN mode.

issue_title: Busmaster wont start in CAN FD mode with ES523 consistently
author: nostar
text_preview: An ES523.1 with the older of the shown firmware versions works fine in CAN-FD mode, but the ES523.1 with the latest firmware will not start in CAN-FD mode.  ![es523_old](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/3847102/12096502/be484856-b2e3-11e5-8b19-18759fa965fb.png)  ![es523_new](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/3847102/12096509/c97bb94c-b2e3-11e5-93f2-552a19145b57.png)

issue_title: Busmaster wont start in CAN FD mode with ES523 consistently
author: nostar
text_preview: If one level of firmware works correctly, and others do not (tried again with even newer firmware from INCA 7.2 with same results), how is this a support issue?

issue_title: Busmaster wont start in CAN FD mode with ES523 consistently
author: nostar
text_preview: The problem is a matter of ISO vs Non-ISO CAN-FD modes.  The older firmware only supports Non-ISO and the newer firmware only supports ISO.  I'm told by ETAS that software switching is a feature coming soon to INCA, so I assume the feature will follow in Busmaster?

issue_title: Busmaster wont start in CAN FD mode with ES523 consistently
author: nostar
text_preview: As of INCA 7.1.10/1 The ability exists to select CAN FD Frame format of ISO or Non ISO.  I am only able to use the ES523 with Busmaster using the desired mode by opening INCA first, selecting the desired mode, and Initializing hardware and leaving INCA open.  Then I can open Busmaster and Connect to the ES523 in the desired mode.  The option to select the CAN FD Frame format needs to be added to the Busmaster BOA driver channel configuration.

issue_title: Main Diagnostic Window not working
author: RBEI-Prathiba
text_preview: Hello, Please use 'CAN->Diagnostics->Settings' window to set 'Diagnostic Standard', 'Interface' and 'CAN ID' values. Source Address and Target Address fields in the 'Main Diagnostic Window' will be enabled only for '11bit Extended' and '29bit' interfaces specified in the 'Settings' window.  If the interface is selected as '11bit Normal', the source address will be taken from 'CAN ID Request to ECU' field in the 'Settings' window. For more details, please refer help 'CAN->Diagnostics' section

issue_title: J1939 Message Handlers are not executed.
author: RBEI-Prathiba
text_preview: Hi, Currently, destination address is considered for broadcast message. Hence, please specify the 'Preferred Address' as '14' for PGN 0xFF14 while creating node in the Node Simulation and check if the handler is executing.

issue_title: J1939 Message Handlers are not executed.
author: NorseMN
text_preview: I think BusMaster is failing to recognize a broadcast proprietary message. The 29-bit identifier used in J1939 is structured in the following way. Priority = 3 bits Reserved = 1 bit Data page = 1 bit PDU Format (PF) = 8 bits PDU Specific (PS) = 8 bits Source Address (SA) = 8 bits  In general: -  When PS = FF, it's a broadcast message. -  Otherwise, it's (mostly) a destination specific message and PS is a destination address (DA)  But, there are also proprietary messages that are either destinati

issue_title: passing data between DLLs
author: nostar
text_preview: For now my solution is to use C file I/O to open a binary file, repeatedly seek to 0, and overwrite the same 2 bytes over and over every 100ms from the CAN node as the message handler is called.  I open the same file from the LIN node as read-only and read it every 100ms or so (as needed). issue_title: Destination address representation is wrong in message window for PDU 2 format messages.
author: rpamparthi
text_preview: The PDU format (PF) determines whether the message can be transmitted with a destination address or if the message is always transmitted as a broadcast message.  The interpretation of the PDU specific (PS) field changes based on the PF value:  If the PF is between 0 and 239, the message is addressable (PDU1) and the PS field contains the destination address. If the PF is between 240 and 255, the message can only be broadcast (PDU2) and the PS field contains a Group Extension. The Group extension

issue_title: Spurious CAN messages displayed
author: RBEI-Prathiba
text_preview: Messages with ID (0x6A04, 0x7204, 0x7A04, ...) are the error messages received from the hardware. BusMaster follows convention for error ids as (0x6A04, 0x7204, 0x7A04, ...), hence the error message with the same id will not be seen in CANOe.

issue_title: Ackownledge management
author: bluehangel
text_preview: Add Ackownledge management in CAN section/settings.

issue_title: Ackownledge management
author: bluehangel
text_preview: CAN Extended frame format ACK slot: The acknowledge slot is used to acknowledge the receipt of a valid CAN frame. Each node that receives the frame without finding an error, transmits a dominant level in the ACK slot and thus overrides the recessive level of the transmitter. If a transmitter detects a recessive level in the ACK slot it knows that no receiver found a valid frame. A receiving node may transmit a recessive to indicate that it did not receive a valid frame, but another node that did

issue_title: Busmaster ver 2.6.1
author: RBEI-Prathiba
text_preview: Hello, Message Type in the transmit window is populated based on the PF value in the PGN. 1) If the PF value in PGN is in the range 0 to 239 (0x00 to 0xEF), message type can only be either 'Request PGN' or 'Data'. 2) If the PF value in PGN is in the range 240 to 255 (0xF0 to 0xFF), message type can only be either 'Request PGN' or 'Broadcast'.  Please input the PGN value within the range to use message type Broadcast. For more details, please refer http://www.kvaser.com/en/about-can/higher-layer-

issue_title: Make J1939 mode as functional as CAN mode
author: glafayette615
text_preview: It seems to me that most of the features available in CAN mode (i.e. 11 bit identifiers) are not available in J1939 mode.  For me, and I assume many others this is a severe limitation of this tool.  Am I missing something?  Is there a workaround method to send J1939 messages, log them into a graphical signal, etc?  Is there an idea of how big the undertaking would be to make the J1939 mode function the same way as the CAN?

issue_title: Make J1939 mode as functional as CAN mode
author: RBEI-Prathiba
text_preview: Hi, J1939 uses the 29-bit identifier defined in the CAN 2.0B protocol. To send J1939 messages please do the following: 1. Activate J1939 using J1939->Activate 2. To configure messages for transmission, use J1939 Transmit Window via menu 'J1939->Transmit'  Currently you can only log the data in to a file using menu 'J1939->Logging'. For more details, please refer section 'J1939' in help file.

The problem is that busmaster simulated node refuses to send a 16bit signal at byte index 4 (which would be 3 and 4), instead it transmit the signal data on byte 4 and byte 5, so it overwrites half of the following signal. issue_title: Node Simulation Big Endian
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: All GUI functions properly handle big/little endian. But the node simulation is not. This is due to the used struct, which is always in machine (=intel=little) endianess. We currently discuss on how to improve this.  As a workaround you can declare a local variable, which is a copy of the message structure passed through htonl. Then you should be able to access this second structure for big endian signals. But watch for padding bits at the end of the structure. Some more hints are here: - http:/

issue_title: Change Encoding of Test-Automation-Setup.xml to UTF-8

text_preview: Most XML-Files are encoded in UTF-8, thus Busmaster should also create UTF-8-Files. To reach this goal it is necessary, to specify the encoding when creating the file. issue_title: Gateway
author: Stefanuccio
text_preview: I would like to know if there's a way to setup a gateway transmission by the software , through can case . So that , income message on can case will be completely substitute with the one defined by the user, and then sended to the network.

issue_title: Gateway
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: Yes. CANcase is supported via the hardware driver "Vector XL". You have to put BUSMASTER in front of your ECU, so that any traffic to and from the ECU is forwarded through BUSMASTER. You can then write a node simulation program in C++ to do the forwarding resp. the manipulation of selected frames and signals.

issue_title: It should be possible to link an external *.dll in a NodeSimulation like the Vector CANalyzer Tool does it with "Extensions CAPL dll´s "
author: RBEI-VENKAT
text_preview: DllMake will be generated every time the dll was build based on the template file GCCDLLMakeTemplate_CAN distributed along with BUSMASTER Installer.. So, to link the library please edit the template provided in {BUSMASTER_InstallerPath}/GCCDLLMakeTemplate_CAN In this case the Template should be, --- #Make file for building DLL DllFile.dll : ObjFile.o @mingw32-g++.exe -shared -Wl,--dll ".o" -o ".dll" "\SimulatedSystems\OBJ\libWrapper_C

issue_title: BUSMASTER v2.0.0 cannot start up on Japanese Windows PC
author: etas-japan-yamamoto
text_preview: BUSMASTER v2.0.0 cannot start up on Japanese Windows PC if "\BUSMASTER_v2.0.0\JPN" folder exists. If the folder was renamed BUSMASTER starts up in English mode. At least the issue occurs on Win7 64bit Japanese machine, other types of OS are not tested.

issue_title: BUSMASTER v2.0.0 cannot start up on Japanese Windows PC
author: etas-japan-yamamoto
text_preview: V210 has the same error.  Japanese customers want to use the latest version in Japanese mode.

issue_title: BUSMASTER v2.0.0 cannot start up on Japanese Windows PC
author: etas-japan-sakurai
text_preview: Same issue is still available at V2.5.0.  The workaround (=removal/rename of "JPN" folder) works. If all Japanese PC cannot run BusMaster, there's no reason to add "JPN" folder in the package, I think. My proposal is just to remove it until fixing it.

issue_title: BUSMASTER v2.0.0 cannot start up on Japanese Windows PC
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: Yes, I agree that this is reasonable. I'll discuss this with my developers today.

issue_title: BUSMASTER v2.0.0 cannot start up on Japanese Windows PC
author: etas-japan-sakurai
text_preview: ### CORRECTION  V2.5.0 is trying to show some of items in menu (ex. Properties etc.) in Japanese, even if "JPN" folder was removed. So I rolled back to V2.4.0 -- it's fine now. Menu has been shown in English. Therefore, please be careful: just removing "JPN" folder is not really good option. (Mr. Lorenz: Thank you for your immediate response.)

issue_title: BUSMASTER v2.0.0 cannot start up on Japanese Windows PC
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: I've just discussed this with my developers. A fix is planned. Instead of removing the JPN folder we can also give these "Language Packs" as installer option. So customers can choose whether this should be installed or not. The same is actually true for German language pack as well.

issue_title: BUSMASTER v2.0.0 cannot start up on Japanese Windows PC
author: etas-japan-sakurai
text_preview: Just FYI -- V2.6.1 works on my Japanese version of Windows 7 64bit, without the workaround above (=removal/rename of "JPN" folder), with Japanese Language Pack which was automatically installed together.

text_preview: 1- Install MinGW.  2- After installation is complete,then Right-click on "My Computer" icon. issue_title: Support for multiplexed signals in CAN messages
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: At least in CAN multiplexed signals are used quiet often. This is currently not supported and hinders some customers to use BUSMASTER.  Background: In a CAN message a signal can be defined as multiplexor. This multiplexor selects which multiplexed signal is transmitted in the same frame.  The CANdb formats supports multiplex configurations.

issue_title: Direct DBC support
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: I propose to implement direct support for DBC format without intermediate DBF format.   Support for data handling usually consists of a data model and load/save functionality from/to the data model. The data model should already be very similar for DBC and DBF, so can probably remain unchanged. Only the load/save functionality must be rewritten.  Advantages: - We can get rid of the DBC>DBF>DBC format converts. This reduces our maintenance efforts. - The user experience improves as no format conv

issue_title: dbf format description?
author: ebroecker
text_preview: Is there any description of the .dbf-fileformat? Does .dbf support multiple transmitters of the same frame (BO_TX_BU_ in .dbc)?  Background of this question is my attempt to write some universal canmatrix-utility (https://github.com/ebroecker/canmatrix) which supports among other features the conversion between several canmatrix-formats (dbc, dbf, kcd, xls, arxml).

issue_title: dbf format description?
author: RBEI-Saravanan
text_preview: Multiple transmitters support for the same frame is not available at present.   Following is the DBF file format assuming the version is 1.7.0 and a CAN database file.  //*****************************_BUSMASTER Messages and signals Database ***_**************************//  [DATABASE_VERSION] 1.3  [PROTOCOL] CAN  [BUSMASTER_VERSION] [1.7.0] [NUMBER_OF_MESSAGES] 1  [START_MSG] Message Name, Message ID, Length, Number of Signals, Data Format, Frame Format [START_SIGNALS] Signal Name, length, byte

issue_title: dbf format description?

text_preview: Multiplexed messages are not yet supported. This feature request, also mentioned in issue #587 and issue #676, is still open.

issue_title: Maximum value of the PGN for J1939 messages
author: bentearadu
text_preview: Maximum PGN for J1939 messages should be FFFF, not 3FFFF, due to the fact the last 2 bites from the byte 3 are not used, (values 3, 2, 1, 0 does not have any effect on the final ID)  See SAE J1939. EDP should be 0 and also DP should be 0 in order for the CAN 29 Bit to be J1939 based.

issue_title: Maximum value of the PGN for J1939 messages
author: RBEI-Prathiba
text_preview: Maximum PGN for J1939 messages is 3FFFF as PGN is of 18bit. Please refer BUSMASTER help "J1939" Section for more details about PGN.

issue_title: the graph window configuarotion -buffer size 50000 at most
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: Answer 1: Yes, currently the graph data is kept in a buffer, which is not a ring buffer. So if it's full, the graph is reset. In my opinion, instead of changing the buffer size, we should find a way to implement a ring buffer.  Answer 2: IMO the data is stored whenever data is received. This is a useful behavior. Setting a cycle time for the capture is a new feature.

issue_title: Cstring in busmaster
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: BUSMASTER is currently based upon MFC (version 4.2 in the Express/WinDDK version). This is an technology is not state-of-the-art and hinders migration onto other platforms (embedded, linux, ...). Therefore there is the intention to migrate the backend non-GUI functionality from MFC to C++11. This is especially regarding several C... classes, not only CString.

issue_title: Cstring in busmaster
author: liguangcheng
text_preview: oh woo, I understand ,but what should I do? replace parts of file with what? I still need a help

issue_title: Cstring in busmaster
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: Sorry. Now I understand your question. We use WinDDK to add the MFC functionality missing from the Visual Studio Express versions. The MFC however is old and contains several bugs. Therefore we provide a patch file that automates the process to patch your WinDDK/MFC installation. Please have a look at "Documents/1 Development Environment/files/WinDDK_7600.16385.1_patch.bat". This should apply all mentioned changes in one step. Does this help?

issue_title: Cstring in busmaster
author: liguangcheng
text_preview: oh, run the path file can apply all mentioned changes that descripted in document file in one step,is that ture? when I build the project there are many many errors,oh my gord!

issue_title: Cstring in busmaster

issue_title: File Converter: Convert multiple files.
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  If organizations change to BUSMASTER, there should be a possibility to convert multiple DBC files to their corresponding DBF file at a time. So it should be possible to select a directory, in which all found DBC files in all subdirectories gets converted. This applies to other converters as well.

issue_title: Log File Configuration: Selectable time before start trigger and after stop trigger
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  It would be nice to provide options to select the time in which messages are logged to file before a start trigger or after a stop trigger hit. This is to know the vehicle activities before and after the trigger condition happened.

issue_title: Log File Configuration: Trigger based on signal content
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  Our log file configuration has the possibility to define a start trigger and a stop trigger. It would be beneficial to provide an option that triggers not only on the message, but also on its signals.

issue_title: Database Editor: Message sorting
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  It would be nice to have a sort option for the message tree. This should either go by the name (sort order as in the signal graph configuration) or by the ID (as it is today).

issue_title: Database Editor: Signal layout
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  The Database Editor the message editing lacks a signal layout box, similar to the one we already have in the Transmission Window.

issue_title: Database Editor: Drag&Drop of Signals
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  It would be beneficial to have means to drag&drop signals from one message to another. Currently such messages need to be copied manually with all parameters.

issue_title: Database Editor: Signals in the tree
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  Our Database Editor only shows messages in the tree view in the left pane. It would be nice to have the signals therein too.

issue_title: Put filter configuration in log file
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  It would be interesting to put the filters applied to the log file configuration, also as information into the log files itself. This is to give a clue why specific information is "missing" therein.

issue_title: PLAY/PAUSE feature in Message Window
author: Arunkumar-Karri
text_preview: It would be nice to have a feature where user can control the message window with a Play/Pause option.   By default, Message window will be in Play mode. When Paused, Message window will not update with incoming messages and show only the existing messages up-to that point.

issue_title: Support DSRC protocol
author: RBEI-Srinivas
text_preview: Dedicated Short Range Communication (DSRC) offers vehicle-to-vehicle (v-v) and vehicle-to- infrastructure (v-i) communications between automobiles. The communication between On-board unit(OBU) and roadside unit (RSU) is wireless and the packets exchanged have to be monitored.

author: Arunkumar-Karri
text_preview: Error Logger is not available in BUSMASTER. Alternatively, use the log file to write any user specfic data at run time. An API 'UINT WriteToLogFile ( char\* msg )' is available in node simulation API which can be used to log data into log files.

issue_title: Truncated strings with COM API WriteToLogFile

issue_title: Feature request: masking in databases
author: SperkLabs
text_preview: Some protocols have fields in either the CAN ID or the message data that determine message type. Also, some protocols have fields in the ID that do not determine message type, such as priorities and addressing.  For J1939, part of this is handled by using a J1939 database. However, some proprietary J1939 messages use the first 1 or 2 bytes to further specify the message type, and then different message types need to be parsed differently.  I propose that for databases, masks (care / don't care)

issue_title: Feature request: masking in databases
author: noblevirk
text_preview: I also support the above proposal. I am currently using controllers that uses propriety format, in which same PGN is used for multiple messages. The messages are differentiated by the priority and sender. In addition to signals within a message can be further identified based upon the byte1 and byte2 values.  I tried implementing the J1939 based database, but it classify multiple messages as one type, even when they have different priority and command bytes.

issue_title: Feature request: masking in databases
author: alesiem
text_preview: I would rate this major improvement since it is important if you work with dynamic node addresses that are part of the CAN ID.   Please have add this feature.

text_preview: After the Message Replay Window is closed, there is no way to have it back.   Restarting the tool, creating different types of replays are not helping reappearing the Message Replay Window  Installed Busmaster Version : 1.7.1 OS Windows: 7

issue_title: Message Replay Window not longer available
author: RBEI-Saravanan
text_preview: In interactive replay method, the functionality of close button is basically stopping the replay. Hence the window will be closed and immediately the replay will be stopped.   To get back the replay window, just disconnect and connect to the network again. No restart of application is required. Make sure, Interactive Replay Window option is selected in the Replay configuration window.

issue_title: Error duplicated mesages
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: As discussed during our joint telco today.  Similar to include guards (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Include_guard), we should enclose each message definition with a "message guard": #ifndef MessageName_xxx #define MessageName_xxx typedef union ... typedef struct ... #endif /\* MessageName_xxx */  xxx should be a hash function for everything that is enclosed (message name, signals, types, ...).  That way we prevent identical messages contained in more than one database to create problems during t

issue_title: Error duplicated mesages
author: Arunkumar-Karri
text_preview: Fix for this issue is available with v1.7.3.

issue_title: Union.h files overwritten
author: bentearadu
text_preview: The files union.h are created each time Busmaster starts.  Due to this, any previous manual changes in these files are lost.  Installed Busmaster Version : 1.7.1 OS Windows: 7

issue_title: Union.h files overwritten
author: RBEI-Saravanan
text_preview: Unions.h file generated is intended to use with BUSMASTER only. So any manual modification will not be retained. If any additional changes or definitions are required then the same can be achieved by creating a new header file and referring it in the cpp file or modifying in the database file itself.

issue_title: Union.h files overwritten
author: bentearadu
text_preview: This issue was raised because because of the error described in issue 398.  After the error from issue 398, is manually removed (the duplication of definitions of typedef) the new database is overwritten when Busmaster is restarted. Due to this, the issue from 398 must be removed manually again.  Removing the issue 398, will automatically solve also this one

issue_title: Union.h files overwritten
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: We should add a file header saying that "This is an automatically generated file based on the database ...".  We should also give a recommendation: "It is recommended to change the database directly. If you want to prevent overwritten header files, make a copy of it and ensure you include the intended version in your node simulation.".  Would that solve the issue in addition to #398?

It's not difficult to fix it, however it's the amount of warnings that still needs time to process.  In addition we are in the progress of converting MFC classes to STL classes. This will help in the end to become platform independent and to enhance the user interface.  We appreciate any help that we can get ;-)  Coming back to the issue: AFAIK building a debug

issue_title: Cannot build a debug version
author: RBEI-Saravanan
text_preview: Since Visual Studio Express Edition is used, Debug version is not supported. busmaster_debug is one configuration that is added which enables debug information in the Release build for debugging purpose. We will update development document as well as suggested.

issue_title: Cannot build a debug version

issue_title: Cannot build a debug version

issue_title: #pragma pack missing from header file generated by 1.7.0
author: SperkLabs
text_preview: The header file generated by version 1.6.4 contained #pragma pack lines. The header file generated by 1.7.0 does not.  A message containing a 16-bit value that was not aligned on a 16-bit boundary was not formed correctly by the new simulated system. After I added the #pragma pack lines by hand and rebuilt it, it worked correctly.

issue_title: #pragma pack missing from header file generated by 1.7.0
author: RBEI-Saravanan
text_preview: GCC displays the below arning :  "warning : malformed pragma pack(pop[, id])' - ignored [-Wpragmas]"  This pack was removed considering this issue. Have you used "#pragma pack(pop,8)" to make the file compile properly ?  If this is the case, we can introduce again the same.   We are using GCC 4.6.1 version.  Please let us know the GCC version which you are using.

issue_title: #pragma pack missing from header file generated by 1.7.0
author: SperkLabs
text_preview: I have GCC 4.6.2 right now. I don't recall what version I had before, I think it was 3.x,y something, and I got that warning.  Yes, I have used "#pragma pack(push,8)" and "#pragma pack(pop,8)", and it results in the "malformed pragma..." warning you listed above. After I read http://gcc.gnu.org/onlinedocs/gcc/Structure_002dPacking-Pragmas.html, it seems that the pop does not need the 8; it's just an argument required for the push.  So now I have manually added "#pragma pack(push,8)" and "#pragma

issue_title: #pragma pack missing from header file generated by 1.7.0
author: SperkLabs
text_preview: Sorry, I probably shouldn't have closed this issue, since it's still present, and many databases won't work correctly without the #pragma pack.

issue_title: #pragma pack missing from header file generated by 1.7.0

issue_title: Busmaster can not find ValueCAN3 if serial# >120000
author: ScottGarabedian
text_preview: Tested two ValueCAN3's with different serial numbers, one below 120000 and one above 120000.  Also tested two different laptops with two different versions of Busmaster (1.6.5 and 1.7.0).  Both versions could find the ValueCAN3 with serial number below 120000, but not above 120000.  Both laptops were Windows 7, 64-bit.

issue_title: Busmaster can not find ValueCAN3 if serial# >120000
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: There were also reports with serial numbers >39999.  This problem is caused by older versions of the Intrepid driver.  BUSMASTER delivers the driver in an older version. To get rid of them, delete the icsneovi.dll and the etasneovi.dll from the applications folder. Then the system-wide installed versions are used.  There is another advantage: The user can download/upgrade/install the drivers independently of BUSMASTER.  As we have similar issues with all other drivers as well, I proposed to drop

issue_title: Remove APIs that are installed by the driver setups anyway
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: We currently deliver several vendor APIs as part of the BUSMASTER installation in %ProgramFiles%\BUSMASTER. Each on its own is not sufficient for the hardware access, but an additional Driver Setup/Installation is always necessary.  APIs and drivers are all available as separate and often combined installers from the vendors nowadays. They install versions on the system that are often more modern than the ones we deliver with BUSMASTER today. They can also be upgraded/downgraded by the user more

With Single and Dual channel devices.  Intrepid have also given

issue_title: Intrepid ValueCAN Serial number > 10000
author: RBEI-Saravanan
text_preview: This error will be displayed when the driver is detected successfully but the hardware is not detected.  Could you please check in the device manager whether the device is detected and listed ? Also please reinstall the hardware drivers and check once again.  There is no change in BUSMASTER in hardware detection.

issue_title: Intrepid ValueCAN Serial number > 10000
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: As described in the hardware support page in the wiki, the hardware selection in BUSMASTER first tries to load ETASneo40.dll, and then icsneo40.dll. So exchanging only the second file is not sufficient. You also have to delete the first file.  Or just select "ETAS BOA". Since BOA v1.4.5 the ES581 is supported.

However, BusMaster just hung when I clicked on the connection button  Paul

author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  Currently Handlers can only be activated or deactivated on a global base, e.g. all Message, Timer, Key, or Error Handlers.  For certain use cases it is beneficial to (de)activate specific Handlers only, e.g. to (de)activate parts of a rest bus simulation on key press.

issue_title: Replay filter entry in menu and button in toolbar
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  For the Message Display Filter and Logging Filter there are menu entries under "Functions->Enable Filter" available, plus corresponding buttons in the toolbar. This is helpful as it shows, whether these filters are active or not. For the Replay Filter this is yet missing. We should add this too.

issue_title: Replay filter entry in menu and button in toolbar
author: RBEI-Saravanan
text_preview: Added replya filter in menuy and toolbar

issue_title: Provide a message counter in the Message Window and Signal Window
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  In certain use cases it is beneficial to have a message counter in the Message Window and the Signal Window. The number increases each time a message with the same id is received.

issue_title: Provide a message counter in the Message Window and Signal Window
author: mauro-ongaro
text_preview: If possible BUSMASTER should also generate a frame counter signal i.e. the signal value is increased by 1 each time a signal is send.

issue_title: Graph Display - Signal Properties on connect
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  Each signal in the Graph Display should be configurable even when connected. This is true for Hide/Disable buttons, line/point type/color.

issue_title: Graph Display - Lines are non-linear at window borders
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  The graph display connects signal measurement points with lines. However on the border the lines are not interpolated to the next point, but is "limited" to the border. So point that are above the upper border are brought down to the border. The same is true for the other three borders.

issue_title: Graph Display - Lines are non-linear at window borders
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: Sorry, not related to #303 as signal graph display != waveform graph display

issue_title: Graph Display - Lines are non-linear at window borders
author: RBEI-Saravanan
text_preview: Fixed with version 1.7.2

issue_title: Graph Display - Absolute/System Time Selection
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  Currently the graph display only shows the absolute time since connect on the X-axle. It should be possible to also set the system time.

issue_title: MHS TinyCAN Controller Configuration
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  The controller configuration dialog for MHS TinyCAN allows to configure either the bit rate or a BTR register value. We should make this clear by a radio button to select between these two options.  We should also document which values for BTR are valid or for which controller they are.

issue_title: MHS TinyCAN Controller Configuration
author: Arunkumar-Karri
text_preview: Fix for this issue is available with v1.7.3.

issue_title: Function Editor - Single Click Selection
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  It is currently not intuitive that a double click is necessary to edit a function body. A single click should be sufficient.

issue_title: Function Editor - Single Click Selection

issue_title: Improvements to "Hex/Dec" toolbar button

text_preview: This is based on customer feedback. 1. The Hex/Dec button should per default set to Hex. 2. There are certain windows that take the Hex/Dec button into account, e.g. Message Display, Tx Messages. This should be extended to all other relevant windows incl. Database Editor, Test Setup Editor, etc.

issue_title: Hot Key Configuration dialog
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  There should be a window to configure the hot keys throughout the tool. Especially when on test drives it is beneficial as touchpad are more complicated to use than F-keys.

issue_title: Prevent hardware selection dialog if no change during restarts
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: This is based on customer feedback.  The configuration file should contain any user-configurable information including the hardware interface and hardware selection. If this information doesn't change during restarts, it is not necessary that the hardware selection dialog shows up again.

issue_title: Prevent hardware selection dialog if no change during restarts
author: Arunkumar-Karri
text_preview: Hello Tobias,  The hardware selection dialog will be prevented in the following two scenarios.  1. During BUSMASTER restart as suggested. 2. When loading an existing configuration which has some hardware interface already selected.

issue_title: Prevent hardware selection dialog if no change during restarts
author: RBEI-Saravanan
text_preview: Channel selection done previously and saved in configuration will be taken by default when the cfx file is loaded. Solved with the commit :+1:9bd1b70

issue_title: Problem with database editor and Big Endian signals
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: When defining a signal in the database editor with more than 8 bits and Big-Endian, BUSMASTER gives a warning "End bit of signal is out of message length".  Reproducable as follows: Setup a CAN message with 8 Byte Length, Standard Frame. Add a signal with Type "unsigned int", Byte Index: 0, Start Bit: 0, Length > 8, Byte Order: "Motorola (Big-Endian)".

issue_title: Problem with database editor and Big Endian signals
author: Arunkumar-Karri
text_preview: Hi,  For a Motorola (Big-Endian) signal, the byte order starts in reverse. i.e if message length is 8, and a Big-Endian signal with 64 bit length has to be added, the Byte index should be set to 7 instead of 0.

issue_title: Problem with database editor and Big Endian signals
author: etas-lorenz
text_preview: Ah, correct. Or if a put the signal on the first two bytes, the lowest start bit is 8. Closing this issue...

author: mauro-ongaro
text_preview: On an unsigned 8 bit signal I have Offset=0 and Factor=0.2.  On the --Configuration Transmission Messages-- window when I write e.g. 5 on the --Phisical Value-- box Busmaster changes it to 4.8 and writes 18 on the --Raw Value-- box (and on CAN bus).  In general when I write X on the --Phisical Value-- box Busmaster changes it to (X-0.2).  If I write e.g. 19 on the --Raw Value-- box Busmaster writes correctly 5 on the --Phisical Value-- box

issue_title: No RTR frames in the database?
author: josch222
text_preview: In the database it is not possible to define RTR messages. I have an  TX-RTR message which is answered by the DUT with a message that has the same identifier. It should be possible to define 2 messages with a different name but same identifier, but only if one of them is a RTR message.  To make it a bit more complicated: The RTR has a DLC >0 but zero databytes are sent. The DLC represents the length of the triggered response message.
